generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor collecting raw 3D point cloud data for environmental perception in autonomous navigation. Part of the 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle's sensor suite for ACC applications."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera providing stereo vision and depth mapping for environmental perception. Used alongside LiDAR for redundant sensor fusion in autonomous navigation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board sensor suite collecting vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation. Directly acquired for real-time monitoring and control feedback in the Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from the Physical Twin Entity to Digital Twin Entity for real-time processing. Includes 2D/3D point clouds converted into actionable tracks via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw depth camera data transmitted from the Physical Twin Entity to Digital Twin Entity. Provides stereo vision and depth mapping for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data (speed, position, orientation) transmitted from the Physical Twin Entity to Digital Twin Entity for real-time monitoring and control validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Actuation feedback data (motor, steering, servo commands) transmitted from the Physical Twin Entity to Digital Twin Entity for validating physical system responses."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VedderElectronicSpeedController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component pre-processing raw LiDAR data to generate object tracks and environmental representations. Performs clustering and tracking to extract meaningful object representations from 2D/3D point clouds."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, NVIDIAJetpackEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation. Used for real-time monitoring, control decisions, and visualization in the Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR point cloud data converted into actionable object tracks and environmental representations. Used for perception tasks in autonomous navigation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel
]

instance DepthCameraDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed depth camera data providing stereo vision and depth mapping for environmental perception. Used for redundant sensor fusion in autonomous navigation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ActuationFeedbackDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuation feedback data validating physical system responses to motor, steering, and servo commands. Used for control validation and system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ACCControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) model implemented in MATLAB/Simulink for closed-loop validation. Co-simulated with the Digital Twin for real-time control decisions and dynamic obstacle avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) middleware enabling inter-process communication between sensors, actuators, and AI modules. Facilitates modular and real-time communication within the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance NVIDIAJetpackEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK enabling CUDA-accelerated AI inference on the Jetson Orin Nano. Optimizes TensorRT models for real-time perception tasks like object detection."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation component serializing and aligning message types for compatibility across the system. Performs unit conversions (e.g., sensor-specific formats to standardized units) for all data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink toolchain for model-based design (MBD) of ACC algorithms. Supports co-simulation with the Digital Twin for closed-loop validation and edge case testing."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance WiFiCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11) communication protocol enabling low-latency (10ms) inter-entity communication between the Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity. Supports remote management commands and OTA updates."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time acquisition and aggregation of ACC-relevant data (LiDAR point clouds, depth camera feeds, vehicle velocity, inter-vehicle distance). Enables centralized logging and anomaly detection (e.g., sudden deceleration, sensor failures)."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCRelevantDataInsight, SensorAnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic workload distribution between the physical twin (Jetson Orin Nano) and the Digital Twin’s cloud/edge infrastructure. Offloads AI-based perception tasks (e.g., object detection, path planning) while retaining low-latency control loops."
    DTDFVocab:provides AIPerceptionOffloadingInsight, ResourceOptimizationAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Teleoperational commands (e.g., emergency braking, route adjustments) executed via Wi-Fi-based communication. Supports OTA updates for firmware and fail-safe interventions (e.g., forcing a halt if LiDAR detects an obstacle)."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlInsight, FailSafeInterventionAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ACCRelevantDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Centralized logging and aggregation of ACC-relevant data streams (LiDAR, depth camera, vehicle state) for real-time monitoring and analysis."
]

instance SensorAnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated detection and alerting of anomalies in sensor data (e.g., sudden deceleration, sensor failures). Triggers corrective actions or user notifications."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AIPerceptionOffloadingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic workload distribution insights for AI-based perception tasks, optimizing resource utilization between edge and cloud infrastructure."
]

instance ResourceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated optimization of computational resources by offloading AI tasks to cloud/edge infrastructure while maintaining low-latency control loops."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Teleoperational insights enabling remote management of the autonomous vehicle, including emergency interventions and route adjustments."
]

instance FailSafeInterventionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated fail-safe interventions (e.g., emergency braking, system halt) triggered by LiDAR or sensor anomalies to ensure safety."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller managing steering and speed via actuation components. Converts desired speed inputs into motor voltage commands for the lower chassis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationFeedbackDataTransmitted
]

// C1: Physical Twin Entity (Environment)
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity representing the 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with onboard sensors, actuators, and computational hardware (Jetson Orin Nano). Hosts the lower-level chassis (mechanical base) and upper-level chassis (computational/sensor modules)."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor, VedderElectronicSpeedController
]

// C1: Digital Twin Entity (Environment)
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin Entity representing the digital mirror of the Physical Twin Entity. Hosts software components for data processing, visualization, and decision-making (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, 3D Visualization)."
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel, VehicleStateDataModel, LiDARPointCloudDataModel, DepthCameraDataModel, ActuationFeedbackDataModel, ACCControlModel
]

// C1: User Entity (Environment)
instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User Entity providing human-machine interfaces for monitoring, analysis, and direct control of the Physical Twin via the Digital Twin Entity. Includes 3D Visualization and Control Panel subcomponents."
    base:contains ThreeDVisualizationComponent, ControlPanelComponent
]

// C1: Components (User Entity)
instance ThreeDVisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "3D Visualization component rendering processed data (e.g., object tracks, vehicle state, projected driving paths) for real-time monitoring. Leverages Presentation FE for pre-processing and display optimization."
]

instance ControlPanelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Control Panel component enabling user interaction for querying DT state, adjusting control parameters, or validating recommendations. Supports dashboards, APIs, or command-line interfaces."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the transmission of control commands from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity**, enabling real-time adjustments to the vehicle’s behavior. Key control flows include:
1. **Command Types**:
   - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) commands**: Enabling/disabling ACC, modifying desired speed, or triggering emergency braking.
   - **Actuation commands**: Direct control signals for motor, steering, and servo systems (e.g., throttle adjustments, steering angle corrections).
2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - Commands are serialized and translated via the **Data Translation** component to ensure compatibility with physical system interfaces.
   - The **Actuation FE** processes these commands to generate executable control signals (e.g., PWM for motor actuation).
3. **Validation & Authentication**:
   - Implicit validation occurs during data translation (e.g., unit conversion checks, message type alignment).
   - No explicit authentication mechanisms (e.g., digital signatures) are described in the document.
4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - Physical system responses (e.g., motor RPM, steering angle) are fed back to the **Digital Twin Entity** via sensor data streams, closing the control loop.
   - The **Actuation FE** monitors feedback to confirm command execution (e.g., verifying motor speed matches the commanded value).
5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the document. Potential conflicts (e.g., competing ACC and manual steering commands) may be resolved via priority-based arbitration within the **Controlling FE**.
6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - Confirmed through sensor feedback loops (e.g., LiDAR tracks validating steering corrections) or explicit acknowledgments from the **Actuation FE**.
7. **Communication Path**:
   - Commands propagate from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity** over the same **Wi-Fi network** used for sensor data transmission (transport layer protocol unspecified).
   - Intra-entity communication within the **Digital Twin Entity** uses **ROS2** for modular control logic execution (e.g., *Controlling FE* → *Actuation FE*).

**Note**: Specific message formats (e.g., ROS2 message types), validation rules, or conflict resolution strategies are not provided in the document.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter clock synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services but implies real-time constraints for sensor-actuator loops, data pre-processing, and device communication within the **Physical Twin Entity**. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **ROS2-based execution framework**, where each Function Element (FE) is implemented as an individual ROS2 package, ensuring deterministic timing for inter-node communication (e.g., between the *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components within the *User Interface FE*)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **entity-based orchestration pattern** derived from ISO 23247 RA, where **Function Elements (FEs)** are mapped to ROS2 packages and deployed across distributed hardware (e.g., FEs for *Physical Twin Entity* on embedded systems, *User Entity* on user-facing devices). Resource management is implicit in the **Physical Twin Entity**, which abstracts inherent system resources (compute, sensors, actuators) and general automotive functions (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). System-wide coordination relies on **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model** for inter-entity communication, with no centralized controller. The architecture deviates from ISO 23247 by **expanding the Physical Twin Entity’s scope** beyond resource-specific functionality to include general automotive logic, potentially impacting **ownership and engineering processes**. The *User Interface FE*’s split into visualization/control subcomponents introduces **modularity** within the *User Entity*, while the unaligned *Data Storage* component suggests **post-hoc integration** outside the standard’s FE hierarchy."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces quantitative fidelity requirements, including a **network latency constraint of at most 100 ms** and **clock synchronization accuracy of 100 ms** to ensure real-time coordination between physical and digital components. Validation and verification methods are not explicitly detailed, but the system incorporates a **human-in-the-loop** approach for monitoring and control. Uncertainty sources, error detection mechanisms, or formal quality assurance processes are not specified in the provided document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed to support real-time communication between entities and software components, with the following specifications:
1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-entity (SW components)**: **ROS2** (Robot Operating System 2) is used for communication between software components within the **Digital Twin Entity** and **Physical Twin Entity**. ROS2 provides a modular, publish-subscribe architecture for data exchange.
   - **Inter-entity (Physical ↔ Digital)**: Communication occurs over a **Wi-Fi network** (transport layer protocol unspecified; the document cuts off at 'using U').
2. **Network Topology & Architecture**:
   - **Decentralized deployment**: The **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity** are deployed on separate hardware components (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson devices), with data flowing between them via dedicated communication channels.
   - **Hierarchical structure**: Sensor data from the **Physical Twin Entity** is aggregated and processed before being transmitted to the **Digital Twin Entity** for visualization and control.
3. **Bandwidth & Latency Requirements**:
   - **Latency**: The system requires **<100ms** end-to-end latency for real-time monitoring and control (implied by the need for ACC and 3D visualization updates).
   - **Bandwidth**: Not explicitly quantified in the document, but high-bandwidth requirements are inferred for LiDAR point cloud transmission (e.g., millions of points per second).
4. **Reliability & Redundancy**:
   - **No explicit redundancy mechanisms** are described (e.g., backup communication paths or failover protocols).
   - Reliability is partially ensured by the **Data Translation** component’s role in validating and aligning message types.
5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not in Document**: No security protocols (e.g., encryption, access control) are mentioned for network communication.

**Note**: The document does not specify:
- The exact transport protocol (e.g., UDP vs. TCP) for inter-entity communication.
- Quality of Service (QoS) settings for ROS2 or Wi-Fi communication.
- Network segmentation or isolation strategies.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is optimized for edge computing, with the following technical details:
1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge deployment**: The system is deployed on **NVIDIA Jetson NX** and **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** devices, which provide the computational power for real-time processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering, 3D visualization).
   - **No cloud or on-premise components** are mentioned; the architecture is hardware-centric and localized.
2. **Computational Requirements**:
   - **High-performance processing**: Required for tasks such as point cloud clustering, object tracking, and 3D rendering (e.g., CUDA-accelerated GPUs on Jetson devices).
   - **Real-time constraints**: The system must handle low-latency operations (e.g., <100ms for ACC control loops).
3. **Storage Requirements**:
   - **External storage**: Used for data collection, reporting, and long-term storage of sensor logs (e.g., LiDAR scans, vehicle state history).
   - **No specific storage media** (e.g., SSD, HDD) or capacity details are provided.
4. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Not in Document**: No explicit scalability strategies (e.g., horizontal scaling, load balancing) are described.
5. **Deployment Models**:
   - **Modular deployment**: Each entity (**Physical Twin**, **Digital Twin**, **User**) is deployed on separate hardware components, with clear separation of concerns (e.g., sensor data acquisition vs. visualization).
   - **ROS2-based modularity**: Software components are deployed as ROS2 nodes, enabling dynamic reconfiguration and updates.
6. **Availability & Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Hardware redundancy**: Not explicitly mentioned, but the use of dedicated Jetson devices for critical functions (e.g., ACC control) implies redundancy in some form.
   - **Fault tolerance**: No explicit mechanisms (e.g., watchdog timers, automatic failover) are described.
   - **Data persistence**: Ensured through external storage for reporting and historical analysis.

**Note**: The document does not specify:
- Use of virtualization (e.g., containers, VMs) or orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes).
- Specific operating systems (e.g., Linux RTOS) or deployment automation frameworks.
- Geographical distribution or multi-node clustering strategies.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system complies with the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference architecture**, which defines:
  - **Entity-Based Architecture**: A standardized decomposition into **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, each with clearly delineated responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: While the standard does not mandate specific protocols, it implies compatibility with **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange between entities, as noted for the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**.
  - **Certification and Compliance**: The framework aligns with **manufacturing digital twin best practices** but does not detail certification requirements (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443). Compliance is inferred through adherence to the **entity-based RA** and **information exchange guidelines** (Part 4 of ISO 23247).
  - **Domain-Specific Specifications**: The standard is tailored for **manufacturing use cases**, covering physical elements (equipment, facilities), processes, and personnel. It does not address sector-agnostic standards (e.g., GAIA-X, IIoT reference architectures) but provides a foundational template for interoperability across manufacturing subdomains."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents reference safety-critical systems in the context of **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** as per **ISO 15622:2018**, which outlines performance requirements and test procedures for such systems. However, no explicit mention of **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles) or its **Automotive Safety Integrity Level (ASIL)** classifications, **Hazard and Risk Assessment (HARA)**, or **fail-safe mechanisms** is found. The **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0** standard is cited for **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services**, specifically to ensure **network latency of at most 100 ms** for service requirements, but does not address **end-to-end security protocols, authentication systems, or threat mitigation strategies**. The **safety-critical nature** of the system is implied by the ACC context, but no technical details on **cybersecurity protocols, access control, or risk assessment approaches** are provided."
]
